parsed_review.summary: CP00-376 (RP12.P04.M05.S17–RP12.P04.M06.S04, risk A, 10 units) is a descriptor-only billing-core pack covering the M05 Permission And Audit Binding tail (6 rows) and M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head (4 rows). Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack-level claim is fully supported: (1) range/risk/unit-count and deliverable+micro-title distribution agree across the plan (closeout-pack-plan.json), baseline, manifest, contract, and code constants; (2) all 7 declared public exports exist and are implemented as pure frozen descriptor builders (service.js:1610/1643, validators.js:2451/2486/2542/2563/2586) re-exported via index.js `export *`; (3) packages/billing/src contains zero runtime/write/IO patterns (no fs/fetch/child_process/process.env/SQL/db); (4) model.test.js:696-724 deep-equals the contract's p04_permission_fixture_slice_descriptor, cp376_requirements, and cp376_no_write_attestation against code constants and asserts descriptor-only rows, claude_is_final_approval=false, production_ready_candidate=true, and the CP00-377 handoff (next RP12.P04.M06.S05); (5) the RP12 validator covers CP00-376. Boundaries hold: manifest production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false (no over-claim and no weakening of production_ready gates), no_write_attestation sets promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, and Hermes is used only for the deterministic H12 descriptor-only `_no_write` evidence receipt (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. CP178+ hardening is satisfied: readiness auth_preflight passed (exit 0, 1436-byte valid JSON), validation-report confirms prompt within size limit / no forbidden tools / read_tools_used, and forbidden_review_evidence/invalid_review_blockers are enumerated. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not the final approver; enterprise trust closure requires human approval (downstream). The 3 post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly still absent at this mid-sequence review stage. One non-blocking P3 cosmetic observation about scope-file classification is noted. No P0/P1/P2 issues; pack and goal closeout are not blocked.

findings.title: changed-file-scope labels pack-owned doc artifacts as both implementation and unrelated-preserved
findings.details: `docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md` and the `docs/closeout-packs/cp00-376/` directory appear simultaneously in `untracked_implementation_files` (line 130-133) and `unrelated_dirty_files_preserved` (line 135-145). The cp00-376/ directory holds this pack's own manifest.json and command-evidence.json, so classifying it as 'unrelated' is inaccurate. The manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest.json:547-555) omits both paths, so the two generated artifacts disagree on classification. Impact is purely cosmetic: both lists are non-destructive (preserve/track), the review scope is correctly driven by active_pack_implementation_refs, and no billing source, test, contract, or production_ready gating is affected.
findings.recommendation: For scope clarity, exclude pack-owned artifact paths (docs/closeout-packs/cp00-376/) from unrelated_dirty_files_preserved in the scope generator, or document that unrelated_dirty_files_preserved is a non-destructive catch-all that may overlap with untracked_implementation_files. No code or runtime change required.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking observation. Does not affect descriptor-only scope, exports, tests, Hermes/local validation evidence, no-write/no-real-data boundaries, or the read-only reviewer authority boundary. Safe to close CP00-376; optionally tidy in the scope-file generator.
